name: Manasa

distinctive_features: Sheltered by the canopy of seven cobra hoods
Sitting on a lotus platform or standing upon a snake
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Upper right hand holds a conch, left hand holds a lotus, lower left hand holds a snake, lower right hand displays Abhayamudra

domain_specific_functional.Role_in_Mythology: Presiding deity of snakes and reptiles

relationships.description: Manasa is the daughter of sage Kashyapa.
Manasa is the sister of Vasuki, king of Nāgas.
Manasa is married to sage Jaratkaru.
Manasa is the mother of the sage Astika.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Bihar, Odisha, Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, Assam, northeastern India
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents fertility, prosperity, and protection from snakebites

contrastive_constraints: NOT without the canopy of cobra hoods
NOT without her golden complexion and red garments
